title: Flow control device and method
abstract: A flow control device having a longitudinal axis includes an outer housing having at least one fluid inlet, a multi-channel flow member positioned radially within the outer housing, a plurality of flow channels formed between the outer housing and the flow member, at least two of the plurality of flow channels having a different flow resistance rating from each other, and a radial window formed in an outlet region of each of the plurality of flow channels. The flow control device further includes a sliding sleeve positioned radially within the multi-channel flow member, the sliding sleeve including a first section of radial slots. The first section of radial slots is configured to align with a selected radial window via longitudinal movement of the sliding sleeve with respect to the multi-channel flow member.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A flow control device (138) having a longitudinal axis (12) and comprising: an outer housing (14) having at least one fluid inlet (18); a multi-channel flow member (20) positioned radially within the outer housing (14), a plurality of flow channels (22) formed between the outer housing (14) and the flow member (20), at least two of the plurality of flow channels (22) having a different flow resistance rating from each other, and a radial window (44) formed in an outlet region of each of the plurality of flow channels (22); and, a sliding sleeve (50) positioned radially within the multi-channel flow member (20), the sliding sleeve (50) including a first section of radial slots (52); wherein the first section of radial slots (52) is configured to align with a selected radial window (44) via longitudinal movement of the sliding sleeve (50) with respect to the multi-channel flow member (20). 2. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein the fluid inlet (18) in the outer housing (14) is screened. 3. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein, when the first section of radial slots (52) is misaligned from each radial window (44), fluid communication between the fluid inlet (18) and an interior (70) of the sliding sleeve (50) is prevented. 4. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein the multi-channel flow member (20) further comprises a by-pass port (42) substantially aligned with the fluid inlet (18), the sliding sleeve (50) longitudinally movable to fluidically communicate the by-pass port (42) with an interior (70) of the sliding sleeve (50). 5. The flow control device (138) of claim 4, wherein the sliding sleeve (50) includes a second section of radial slots (54) longitudinally spaced from the first section of radial slots (52), and the second section of radial slots (54) is configured to align with the bypass port (42) when the first section of radial slots (52) is misaligned from each radial window (44). 6. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein the outer housing (14) and the multi-channel flow member (20) are longitudinally fixed relative to each other. 7. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein each radial window (44) in the at least two of the plurality of flow channels (22) is longitudinally spaced from each other. 8. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein the first section of radial slots (52) is framed by a pair of seal assemblies (56). 9. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, wherein each flow channel (22) has a flow path in an axial direction with unique flow properties relative to other flow channels (22) and wherein only one of the plurality of flow channels (22) is fluidically communicable with an interior (70) of the sliding sleeve (50) at a time. 10. The flow control device (138) of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of longitudinally spaced indicators (80) configured to provide an indication when the first section of radial slots (52) aligns with each radial window (44). 11. A method of controlling flow between an annulus (130) of a borehole (110) and an interior (70) of a tubing (122) comprising: using a flow control device (138) having a longitudinal axis (12); an outer housing (14) having at least one fluid inlet (18), a multi-channel flow member (20) positioned radially within the outer housing (14), a plurality of flow channels (22) formed between the outer housing (14) and the flow member (20), at least two of the plurality of flow channels (22) having a different flow resistance rating from each other, and a radial window (44) formed in an outlet region of each of the plurality of flow channels (22); and a sliding sleeve (50) positioned radially within the multi-channel flow member (20), the sliding sleeve (50) including a first section of radial slots (52); and the first section of radial slots (52) is configured to align with a selected radial window (44) via longitudinal movement of the sliding sleeve (50) with respect to the multi-channel flow member (20); selecting a desired flow resistance rating amongst the plurality of flow channels (22); and, moving the sliding sleeve (50) longitudinally within the multi-channel flow member (20) until the first section of slots (52) aligns with the radial window (44) of a flow channel (22) having the desired flow resistance rating. 12. The method of controlling flow of claim 11, further comprising engaging the sliding sleeve (50) with an indicator (80) when the first section of slots (52) is aligned with each radial window (44). 13. The method of controlling flow of claim 11, further comprising moving the sliding sleeve (50) to misalign the first section of slots (52) with each of the radial windows (44) to prevent fluid communication between the interior (70) of the sliding sleeve (50) and the fluid inlet (18). 14. The method of controlling flow of claim 11, further comprising by-passing the flow channels (22) by aligning a second section of slots (54) with a by-pass port (42) in the multi-channel flow member (20). 15. The method of controlling flow of claim 14, further comprising treating the annulus (130) through the second section of slots (54), by-pass port (42), and fluid inlet (18), wherein treating includes at least one of hydraulic fracturing, stimulation, tracer injection, cleaning, acidizing, steam injection, water flooding, and cementing.
